title: sambucus racemosa subsp. miquelii
intro: Sambucus racemosa subsp. miquelii, also known as the American elderberry, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Adoxaceae. It is native to North America and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Sambucus racemosa subsp. miquelii include its ability to alleviate cold and flu symptoms, boost the immune system, and provide relief from menstrual cramps and other digestive issues. Sambucus racemosa subsp. miquelii is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
